$comment: Machine-readable Kernel Component & Property Standard. Human narrative: STANDARD.md. Consumed by the catalog gate (grove BACKLOG B-20). Exported as @corilus/kernel/standard.json so every consumer + skill reads one copy.

interactionState.rule: Never a prop. Styled via data-* attributes in CSS.

passThroughControl.rule: Mirror the primitive's prop name verbatim. Booleans are bare (Base UI / HTML form), not is*-prefixed.

dsPresentation.rule: variant + tone + size. The only axis Kernel invents.

content.rule: Slots / children / text props. Trailing/leading, not right/left.

variant.$comment: style/emphasis enum; values defined per component (e.g. Banner: subtle|strong, Btn: primary|secondary)

dsOnly.note: prefer expressing as tone/variant if it is really a style axis

notProps.density: orthogonal token modifier --density-*, applied at ancestor
notProps.radius|elevation|spacing: resolve to tokens at the component class, not props

scope.promotionRule: graduate into Kernel only when a 2nd agent needs it
gateRules: a global component may not import an agents:[...] component (no upward dependency)
two agent-scoped components resolving to the same tokens+anatomy are flagged as a promotion candidate
scope must be present and honest

gateChecks: banned interaction-state props (is*) are an error - state is data-*
non-canonical style synonyms (kind/appearance/intent...) are an error
raw controls (<button>/<input>/...) with no composed Kernel equivalent are an error (false composes)
a composing layer (molecule/composite/organism) with composes:[] is a warning
hand-rolled .is-on className state that should be data-* is a warning (CSS half; the isOpen JS-state half needs a parser Kernel intentionally does not take)
forwardRef present on any component used as a Base UI render target (implemented: flags a component defined in the scanned tree used as `render={<X/>}` without forwardRef)
scope present and not violating gateRules (upward-dependency implemented; cross-agent-drift is greenhouse-audit scope, deferred)
tone present where --status-* is consumed: deferred - the contrast rule lives in the token ramp builder (WCAG-targeted step pairs) and is verified by `kernel doctor`, not re-checked per component

lint.$comment: Data-driven rules consumed by tools/catalog-gate.mjs. Prop-name validation is intentionally OPEN for content/data props (children, items, payload, ...) - the gate errors only on bannedProps + forbiddenStyleSynonyms, never on an unrecognised content prop, to stay false-positive-free.

controlComponents.$comment: the canonical Kernel wrappers for each raw element; a file that DEFINES one of these (its meta key) is exempt from the raw-control check for that tag - that raw element is the primitive itself.

lint.$atomsNote: atoms are leaf primitives and may render a raw control (a button-based row, etc.); only composing layers (molecule/composite/organism) that hand-roll a control instead of composing the atom are flagged. A composing layer mislabelled as an atom is a layer-integrity problem for a later check.

meta.$comment: structured meta shape. Props may be a freeform string (legacy) or the structured object below; gen-catalog + the CLI handle both. Documentation strictness is tiered by `status` (see docTiers).

optionalFields.usage: on-system JSX snippet (string) - the canonical single example
optionalFields.bestPractices: [{ do: boolean, text: string }] - do/don't guidance
optionalFields.anatomy: [{ name: string, required?: boolean, description: string }] - named parts
optionalFields.examples: [{ name: string, code: string, description?: string }] - named code examples. The CLI renders them; each doubles as a Storybook story seed later (reserved slot for Storybook embedding).
optionalFields.storybook: { story?: string, embed?: string } - RESERVED for future Storybook embedding (a ref to the component's stories file / embed config). Carried through the catalog now; not yet rendered.

propShape.name: string
propShape.description: string? - required on Kernel-invented props for stable components; omit for pass-through props (point to Base UI instead)
propShape.passthrough: BaseUI.<Primitive>.<prop>? - marks a pass-through prop; the CLI renders 'see Base UI <Primitive>' instead of a description

docTiers.$comment: Documentation strictness scales with `status`. Enforced by the catalog gate (guard mode).
docTiers.floor: every component (both tiers): a `meta` block and a non-empty `summary` are required (error).
docTiers.stable: official components MUST also carry `usage` and a `description` on every Kernel-invented prop (dsPresentation/content/event/a11y); missing -> error. Promotion to stable is therefore gated on full docs.
docTiers.experimental: proto/snowflake components: usage + prop descriptions are recommended (warning), not required.
